values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 2:

Tests received by maloo, run on el6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/3343035c-68e5-11e4-963a-5254006e85c2).  Ran 2 tests.  1 tests failed: lustre-initialization-1.

Patch Set 2:

Since this patch introduces new lustre-osd-[zfs,ldiskfs]-mount RPMs containing the back-end specific hooks for [mkfs,mount].lustre commands, auto-tests obviously fails at lustre-initialization step.

Patch Set 2:

Could these DSO be built as part of the DKMS lustre-osd-* packages?  I'm just trying to find a way to avoid adding new packages and complexity for the end user.

Patch Set 2:

Andreas: Presently there are no DKMS lustre-osd-* RPMs. The Server lustre-dkms RPM is intended to build the osd_[ldiskfs,zfs].ko modules, and at the moment only creates osd_zfs.ko (--without-ldiskfs) for ZFS only Servers since ldiskfs still requires Kernel to be patched. OTOH, this patch is the 3rd of 3 different I have pushed/proposed to fix problem, and the 1st one (at http://review.whamcloud.com/12538) builds also the DSO as part of lustre-dkms ...
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/28612/

Patch Set 3:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 3: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/28612/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 3:

Tests received by maloo, run on el6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/41f2b7d2-7702-11e4-b1ab-5254006e85c2).  Ran 2 tests.  1 tests failed: lustre-initialization-1.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

lustre-osd-zfs should require lustre-osd-zfs-mount and lustre-osd-ldiskfs should require lustre-osd-ldiskfs-mount otherwise packages won't pull correctly in dependency chains and lustre-initialization will fail

Patch Set 3:

Nathaniel: don't you think the Require order is not that each of the lustre-osd-[ldiskfs,zfs]-mount RPM to require associated lustre-osd-[ldiskfs,zfs] ? I have changed the previous rule in patch-set #3 by adding/specifying the associated back-end.
I have also re-based the patch.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/29158/

Patch Set 4: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/29158/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

In addition to the comments, Autotest also explicitly installs luster-osd-zfs which I should think should correctly pull in lustre-osd-zfs-mount.  So, unless lustre-dkms will be changed to also Provide lustre-osd-zfs, I think the requirements should be osd requires dso.

Patch Set 4:

This latest patch also failed review-zfs due to LU-6054 "mkfs.lustre: FATAL: unhandled fs type 5 'zfs'" which looks like it may be related to this patch.

Patch Set 4:

Nathaniel: I think that to understand your concerns I miss the knowledge about how node-provisioning step works in auto-tests. Can you help and describe it in LU-5851?

parents.subject: LU-6025 utils: reset optind before calls to getopt_long()

commit.subject: LU-5851 build: split lustre-osd RPMs
commit.message:
LU-5851 build: split lustre-osd RPMs

Since Lustre Server DKMS builds osd Kernel modules and Provides
lustre-osd, we need to find a way to also install the
osd-specific dso that is also shipped as part of lustre-osd RPM.

To fix this, now ship the osd-specific dso as part of its own RPM.
And also create its new lustre-osd-mount dependency to ensure
the lustre-osd -> lustre-osd-mount -> lustre coherency and
ordering for Server installs.

Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bruno.faccini@intel.com>
Change-Id: I25d498590212e696f74018c0af694bfee4d1cc81
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/12589
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
